answer:
\boxed{B}  

The study found fetal thrombocytopenia in only 10% of Kell cases, which was lower than the 26% in RhD cases. Additionally, none of the Kell-affected fetuses had clinically significant thrombocytopenia (platelet count <50 × 10⁹/L), even among those with severe hydrops. Therefore, Kell alloimmunization is not associated with a significant risk of fetal thrombocytopenia.
